title: Health endpoint
description: Expose GET /api/health that returns 200 OK with JSON containing status, build version, and timestamp. This is the first vertical slice: it validates the harness, the Spring Boot setup, and the verification pipeline.
acceptance: GET /api/health returns 200 with a JSON body containing 'status', 'version', and 'timestamp'.
A controller test asserts the contract.
./init.sh passes.

title: Core domain models
description: Define the core domain types: Player, Room, Game, Move, GameStatus, Side. Use records for value objects and entities for things that will be persisted later. No persistence yet — these are in-memory types only.
acceptance: Records and enums exist under io.github.dariogguillen.chess.domain.
Unit tests cover invariants (e.g., a Room cannot have more than 2 players).
./init.sh passes.

title: Server-side move validation
description: Integrate the chesslib library. Provide a ChessRules service that, given a current FEN and a proposed move (from-square, to-square, optional promotion), returns the new FEN, whether the move was legal, and the resulting GameStatus (ongoing, check, checkmate, stalemate, draw).
acceptance: ChessRules service has a clear API and JavaDoc.
Tests cover: legal move, illegal move, check, checkmate, stalemate, castling, en passant, promotion.
./init.sh passes.

title: Room REST endpoints (in-memory)
description: REST endpoints to create a room and join a room. State lives in an in-memory store for now (a thread-safe Map). Maps roughly to the Node createRoom and joinRoom events. WebSocket comes later.
acceptance: POST /api/rooms creates a room and returns the room ID.
POST /api/rooms/{id}/join joins an existing room as the second player.
Errors return appropriate HTTP status with a structured error body (room not found, room full, etc.).
Integration tests cover happy path and error cases.
./init.sh passes.

title: OpenAPI spec and Swagger UI via springdoc
description: Adopt springdoc-openapi-starter-webmvc-ui to auto-generate an OpenAPI 3 spec from the existing REST endpoints and mount Swagger UI for interactive exploration. Enrich the spec with @Operation summaries and @ApiResponse declarations so every 2xx and 4xx response is documented honestly, with ErrorResponse as the body schema for error cases. Adopted now (between room-rest-api and game-rest-api) so each subsequent REST feature ships its endpoints with springdoc annotations from day one rather than accumulating retroactive doc churn. WebSocket/STOMP is intentionally out of scope; that surface is documented in the README when feature 6 lands.
acceptance: springdoc-openapi-starter-webmvc-ui is added to pom.xml.
GET /v3/api-docs returns a valid OpenAPI 3 JSON spec covering /api/health, POST /api/rooms, and POST /api/rooms/{id}/join.
Swagger UI is reachable at /swagger-ui.html and renders the three endpoints with their parameters, request/response schemas, and error responses.
Each endpoint declares its 2xx and the relevant 4xx responses via @ApiResponse, with ErrorResponse as the body schema for 4xx.
README documents the Swagger UI URL and /v3/api-docs for client codegen, and trims the manual endpoint table in favor of a link to the spec.
An integration test asserts that /v3/api-docs returns 200 with a payload containing each documented operation and that /swagger-ui.html returns a 200 HTML response.
./init.sh passes.

title: Game REST endpoints (in-memory)
description: REST endpoints to make a move and to query game state. Move requests are validated by ChessRules. Game state lives in the in-memory store.
acceptance: POST /api/games/{id}/moves submits a move; rejects illegal moves with 422.
GET /api/games/{id} returns the current game state (FEN, turn, status, players).
Integration tests exercise a full short game (Fool's Mate or similar).
./init.sh passes.

title: Real-time updates over STOMP
description: Add Spring WebSocket with STOMP. After a move is accepted by the REST endpoint, the new state is broadcast to all subscribers of the game's topic. Subscribers include the opponent and any future spectators.
acceptance: WebSocket configured on /ws with STOMP.
After a successful move, opponents on /topic/games/{id} receive the new state.
Integration test connects two STOMP clients, makes a move, asserts the broadcast.
./init.sh passes.

title: Spectator mode with live viewer count
description: Allow non-player participants to subscribe to a game's STOMP topic and receive live state. The server tracks the current viewer count per game and publishes it so the UI can render it live. TENTATIVE: the exact approach — anonymous STOMP subscribers (no domain change) vs. domain-modeled viewers with identity — is decided during or after feature 6 (websocket-realtime), once the bus is in place and the trade-offs are concrete. The user explicitly wants the viewer count visible regardless of which path is taken.
acceptance: Non-player clients can subscribe to /topic/games/{id} and receive state updates.
Server tracks the current viewer count per game (via STOMP session tracking, domain modeling, or both).
Viewer count is published to subscribers (e.g. on /topic/games/{id}/viewers or as part of the state payload) so the UI can render it live.
Integration test: a viewer subscribes, sees a state update broadcast, the count ticks up; on viewer disconnect the count ticks down.
./init.sh passes.

title: Constrain ErrorResponse.error to the known error code enum
description: Annotate ErrorResponse.error with @Schema(allowableValues = {...}) enumerating the 9 error codes the GlobalExceptionHandler actually emits today (ROOM_NOT_FOUND, ROOM_FULL, GAME_NOT_FOUND, GAME_ALREADY_ENDED, ILLEGAL_MOVE, NOT_YOUR_TURN, VALIDATION_FAILED, MALFORMED_REQUEST, MISSING_HEADER). Springdoc renders this as an enum array in the OpenAPI spec; openapi-typescript on the frontend turns it into a TypeScript union literal automatically. Driven by cross-repo coordination with chess-frontend feature 3 (rest-room-integration). Includes an OpenApiIT canary asserting the enum array matches exactly, protecting against silent drift when a future feature adds a new error code without updating the @Schema.
acceptance: ErrorResponse.error annotated with @Schema(allowableValues = {...}) listing all 9 codes currently emitted by GlobalExceptionHandler.
@Schema description on the field explains its purpose and points to the exception hierarchy.
OpenApiIT extended (or new test) asserting components.schemas.ErrorResponse.properties.error.enum is an array of exactly the 9 expected codes, in a deterministic order.
Audit of GlobalExceptionHandler documented in the feature note: every handler path produces one of the 9 codes; no orphans, no missed codes.
docs/architecture.md API contract section lists the 9 codes with their HTTP status mappings (404/409/422/400).
./init.sh passes.

title: Containerize the backend (Dockerfile + local docker-compose)
description: Multi-stage Dockerfile (Eclipse Temurin JRE Jammy) producing a runnable image of the Spring Boot fat jar. docker-compose.yml for local development bringing up the app + Postgres + Redis as a self-contained stack. application.yml gains env-var-with-default configuration so the same artifact runs locally with Testcontainers, locally with docker-compose, and in production (set in features 7.5 and 7.7). README documents the three local-dev workflows. No CI/CD yet; that's feature 7.7.
acceptance: Dockerfile multi-stage: builder stage with Maven + JDK, runtime stage with Temurin JRE Jammy. Final image runs the Spring Boot fat jar.
Dockerfile includes HEALTHCHECK directive hitting /api/health.
.dockerignore excludes target/, .git/, .claude/, progress/, notes/, IDE files.

docker-compose.yml brings up app + Postgres 16 + Redis 7-alpine. depends_on with healthcheck conditions. Volumes for Postgres data persistence. Host port mappings: 8080 (app), 5432 (postgres), 6379 (redis).

application.yml uses ${SPRING_DATASOURCE_URL:fallback} env-var-with-default pattern for datasource (Postgres) and Redis. Local Testcontainers run still works (TestcontainersConfiguration sets the URLs via @ServiceConnection).
docker compose up --build starts the full stack; /api/health returns 200; /v3/api-docs returns valid OpenAPI 3 JSON.

README.md adds a 'Running with Docker' section documenting three local-dev workflows: (a) ./mvnw spring-boot:test-run with Testcontainers, (b) docker compose up, (c) hybrid (compose for infra + spring-boot:run for app).
docs/architecture.md mentions the container layer briefly as the deployment artifact shape.
./init.sh passes unchanged (no Docker steps added to init.sh; verification of Docker is manual + by feature 7.7's CI smoke test).

title: Provision AWS infrastructure for the backend
description: Provision the AWS Free Tier infrastructure manually first, scripts after: EC2 t3.micro (Ubuntu 22.04), RDS Postgres db.t3.micro, Security Groups, Elastic IP. Set up Caddy as reverse proxy with auto-TLS via Let's Encrypt, pointing at a Duck DNS subdomain. Deploy the Docker image manually (pull from local for now; ECR auth comes in feature 7.7). Set up AWS Budget alarm at $1/month. Document everything in a runbook so the steps can be re-executed (and so feature 7.7's automation has clear targets).
acceptance: EC2 t3.micro running Ubuntu 22.04 in us-east-1, with Docker installed and a non-root user for app operations.
RDS Postgres db.t3.micro in the same region, security group allowing only the EC2 instance's IP.
Elastic IP attached to the EC2 instance.
Duck DNS subdomain (e.g. chess-backend.duckdns.org) pointing at the Elastic IP.
Caddy reverse-proxy on the EC2 instance terminating TLS via Let's Encrypt for the Duck DNS subdomain, proxying to the backend container on port 8080.
Redis self-hosted in a Docker container on the same EC2 (no ElastiCache; Redis is not in AWS free tier).
Backend container running, healthcheck green, /api/health reachable over HTTPS at the public subdomain.
AWS Budget alarm at $1/month with email notification.
ECR repository created (lifecycle policy: keep last 5 images). No push automation yet (that's 7.7).
Runbook in docs/ (or notes/) capturing every manual step taken, with the exact AWS console paths or CLI invocations, so the steps can be replayed or migrated.

title: GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline with OIDC and ECR
description: Automate the deploy. GitHub Actions workflow runs ./init.sh, builds the Docker image, pushes to ECR using OIDC-federated authentication (no long-lived AWS access keys), SSHes into the EC2 instance, and triggers a docker pull + container restart. Includes a post-deploy smoke test against the production /api/health endpoint. Idempotent: pushing the same commit twice is safe.
acceptance: .github/workflows/deploy-backend.yml triggers on push to main.
Job runs ./init.sh; build fails if init.sh red.
AWS OIDC trust configured: IAM identity provider for token.actions.githubusercontent.com, IAM role with trust policy for the repo, scoped permissions (ECR push + minimal else).
GitHub Actions uses aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials with role-to-assume; no AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID secret stored.
Docker image tagged with both the commit SHA and 'latest'; pushed to ECR.
SSH step uses a deploy-specific key (stored in GitHub Actions secret), pulls the new image on EC2, restarts the container via docker compose pull + up -d.
Post-deploy smoke test: curl https://<duckdns-subdomain>/api/health; fail the workflow on non-200.
Concurrency group set so two pushes to main don't deploy in parallel.
README documents the deploy flow with a high-level diagram or sequence.
Feature 12 (github-actions-ci) is re-scoped to PR-only validation workflow (this feature covered the deploy automation).

title: Active game state in Redis
description: Move the in-memory state of active games and rooms to Redis. Active rooms get a TTL (e.g., 24h) so abandoned rooms self-clean. Spring Data Redis with a synchronous template.
acceptance: Rooms and active games are stored in Redis, keyed by room/game ID.
TTL is applied and refreshed on activity.
Existing REST and WebSocket tests still pass, now against Testcontainers-Redis.
./init.sh passes.

title: Game history in Postgres
description: Persist completed games to Postgres for history. JPA entities, Flyway migrations for the schema. Endpoint to list a player's recent games.
acceptance: Flyway migration V1__games.sql creates the games table.
Completed games are saved with players, result, move history.
GET /api/players/{id}/games returns the player's history.
Integration test against Testcontainers-Postgres.
./init.sh passes.

title: Disconnect and reconnect
description: Track player connection state. On disconnect, start a grace period (e.g., 60s). If the player reconnects within the grace period, restore them to the game. If not, mark the game as abandoned.
acceptance: Reconnection within grace period restores game state for the player.
Timeouts mark the game as abandoned with the opponent as winner.
Integration tests cover both paths.
./init.sh passes.

title: Docker Compose for local development
description: Docker Compose file that spins up the app, Postgres, and Redis. Document the workflow in README.
acceptance: docker compose up -d brings up app + postgres + redis on local ports.
README has a 'Run locally' section.
./init.sh passes.

title: CI pipeline on GitHub Actions
description: GitHub Actions workflow that runs ./init.sh on every push and pull request. Caches Maven dependencies.
acceptance: .github/workflows/ci.yml exists and runs ./init.sh.
Build passes on main.
Status badge added to README.
./init.sh passes.

title: Polished README with architecture diagram
description: Final README with: project overview, stack, how to run, architecture diagram (Mermaid), API summary, screenshots if available. This is the artifact a recruiter will read.
acceptance: README explains what the project is and how to run it in under 5 minutes.
Mermaid architecture diagram is rendered correctly on GitHub.
Links to harness engineering files (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md) are present so reviewers can see the workflow.
